授業目的: The purpose of this course is to help students improve their overall English reading comprehension and skills. The course will aim to assist students in building and practicing using some of the fundamental skills needed to develop and improve overall English reading abilities. Students will be introduced to engaging nonfiction reading passages and a variety of activities to improve reading comprehension, critical thinking, and vocabulary knowledge. Students will learn about and be encouraged to use various reading strategies, including activating prior knowledge, skimming, scanning, making inferences, predicting, and summarizing. There will be an emphasis on improving students’ lexical range. Students will expand their vocabulary knowledge and use through an emphasis on learning and understanding target words and phrases.Students will also participate in extensive reading throughout the semester, with regular word count goals.
到達目標: Students will improve overall comprehension of reading passages.Students will learn about and understand the structure and features of paragraphs and reading passages.Students will learn about and develop reading strategies to improve overall reading skills and comprehension, including skimming, scanning, making inferences, predicting, and summarizing.Students will expand their vocabulary knowledge of target words and phrases through a variety of reinforcement activities.Students will improve their critical thinking skills.Students will participate in extensive reading to improve their reading speed and confidence.
授業方法: Course format will be face to face.During class, students will be required to complete work individually and collaboratively. Students will be required to complete extensive reading and self-reflection tasks, outside of class time.

各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。

備考Note0:
Prerequisite: Taking the TOEIC test in December required by KGU is the prerequisite for credit earning.  
Absences: Four absences are considered excessive and result in automatic failure.  Leaving a classroom without asking for permission, doing something else other than class activities, or sleeping are considered an absence. 
Tardiness: Coming into the classroom after the bell is considered tardiness.  Arrival after 30 minutes is considered an absence.  Two tardy arrivals equal one absence.  (Being late is not considered tardiness in case of documented delay of public transportation.)   
Make-up Assessments: Make-up assessments will not be given except in cases of documented medical necessity or family emergency.  
Cheating: Cheating in any quizzes, tests, projects, or assignments results in zero point on the work.  
単位習得条件：大学が義務付けている12月の学内TOEICテストを受験していることが単位習得の前提条件です。 
欠席：4回以上欠席をした場合、その時点で単位習得は不可です。なお、教員に許可を得ず授業中に退室したり、他の科目の作業をしたり、眠ったりしている場合は欠席とカウントします。
遅刻：チャイム後の入室は遅刻です。30分以上の遅刻は欠席とみなします。また、2回の遅刻で1回の欠席とカウントします。（公共交通機関の遅延で到着が遅れた場合は、遅刻のカウント対象外です。）
代替試験および課題：代替試験の受験や課題提出が認められるのは登校停止や親族の死亡の事由の届け出がある場合のみです。
不正行為：小テスト・試験・提出物・宿題などいかなる課題でも不正な行為が認められた場合はその課題の得点はゼロです。
